 {"id":"bd-1jjq","title":"[10.10] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging","description":"## Why This Exists

This is the section-wide verification gate for Section 10.10 (FCP-Inspired Hardening + Interop Integration Track). Section 10.10 hardens the product control plane by adopting canonical trust protocols, deterministic serialization, key-role separation, and interop fixtures from the FCP (Franken Compatibility Protocol) deep-mined tracks (10.13 + 10.14). This gate ensures all 11 hardening beads are validated with comprehensive tests and structured evidence.

Section 10.10 is critical because it bridges the low-level protocol trust infrastructure (10.13/10.14) into product-visible surfaces: trust object IDs, token chains, zone segmentation, error namespaces, and release gates. Without this gate, product surfaces could ship without the hardening guarantees that 10.13/10.14 provide.

## What This Must Do

1. Aggregate verification evidence from all 11 Section 10.10 beads:
   - bd-1l5: Define canonical product trust object IDs with domain separation
   - bd-jjm: Enforce canonical deterministic serialization and signature preimage rules
   - bd-174: Implement policy checkpoint chain for product release channels
   - bd-2ms: Implement rollback/fork detection in control-plane state propagation
   - bd-1r2: Implement audience-bound token chains for control actions
   - bd-364: Implement key-role separation for control-plane signing/encryption/issuance
   - bd-oty: Integrate canonical session-authenticated control channel + monotonic anti-replay framing
   - bd-2sx: Integrate canonical revocation freshness semantics before risky/dangerous actions
   - bd-1vp: Implement zone/tenant trust segmentation policies
   - bd-13q: Adopt canonical stable error namespace and compatibility policy
   - bd-1hd: Adopt canonical trust protocol vectors/golden fixtures as release gates
2. Verify cross-bead integration: trust object IDs are used consistently across token chains, checkpoint chains, and zone segmentation.
3. Verify hardening coverage: every product control-plane API uses session-authenticated channels, anti-replay framing, and revocation freshness checks.
4. Produce deterministic gate verdict.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 11 section beads must have PASS verdicts with complete verification evidence.
- Cross-bead integration tests verify consistent trust object ID usage across all subsystems.
- Hardening coverage audit confirms no product control-plane API bypasses FCP-derived protections.
- Gate verdict is deterministic and machine-readable.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Gate self-test with mock evidence sets.
- Structured logs: GATE_10_10_EVALUATION_STARTED, GATE_10_10_BEAD_CHECKED, GATE_10_10_HARDENING_COVERAGE_AUDITED, GATE_10_10_VERDICT_EMITTED.

## Expected Artifacts

- `scripts/check_section_10_10_gate.py` — gate script with `--json` and `self_test()`
- `tests/test_check_section_10_10_gate.py` — unit tests
- `artifacts/section_10_10/bd-1jjq/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_10/bd-1jjq/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: bd-1l5, bd-jjm, bd-174, bd-2ms, bd-1r2, bd-364, bd-oty, bd-2sx, bd-1vp, bd-13q, bd-1hd, bd-1dpd, bd-2twu
- Blocks: bd-2j9w (program-wide gate), bd-1hf (plan tracker)","notes":"Root-cause addendum: this gate must explicitly validate bd-ac8j with mutual-auth handshake vectors, role-confusion failures, stale-session replay, protected-endpoint inventory audits, and raw endpoint bypass denial attempts. Require at least one deterministic tests/e2e shell scenario plus validation of SESSION_AUTH_* and CONTROL_ENDPOINT_AUTH_* structured logs and endpoint inventory artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:06.665766167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T07:59:31.875528773Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T07:59:31.875489650Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-10","test-obligations","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-13q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-174","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-1dpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-1hd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-1l5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-1r2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-1vp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-2ms","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-2sx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-2twu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-364","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-jjm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","depends_on_id":"bd-oty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":53,"issue_id":"bd-1jjq","author":"RoseMountain","text":"Reopened because the closed 10.10 gate depended on bd-oty, but the product session-auth layer still records key IDs and passes signature strings through without verification. The section verification story is not yet true. Tracked by bd-ac8j.","created_at":"2026-03-09T00:21:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jmq","title":"[11] Contract field: EV score and tier","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 11

Task Objective:
Require EV scoring metadata and tier classification for each major subsystem change.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [11] Contract field: EV score and tier are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [11] Contract field: EV score and tier are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_11/bd-1jmq/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_11/bd-1jmq/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[11] Contract field: EV score and tier" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[11] Contract field: EV score and tier" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every evidence contract includes an EV-score field computed as: EV = P(success) * benefit - P(failure) * cost, with explicit numeric values for each term.
2. Tier classification follows: Tier-1 (EV >= 10, critical path), Tier-2 (EV >= 1, important), Tier-3 (EV < 1, nice-to-have).
3. The EV calculation references concrete data: measured test pass rates, estimated blast radius, historical incident cost.
4. CI rejects contracts where EV score is missing, non-numeric, or tier is inconsistent with the computed EV value.
5. Unit test: contracts with correct EV and tier pass; contracts with mismatched tier/EV or missing fields fail validation.
6. A helper script or library function exists to compute EV from input parameters and assign tier automatically.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:32.649250887Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:18:24.782882282Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:18:24.782855983Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-11","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jmq","depends_on_id":"bd-36wa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jpc","title":"[10.21] Implement unified evolution-risk scorer with explainability contract and confidence decomposition.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.21 — Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)

Why This Exists:
BPET longitudinal phenotype intelligence track for pre-compromise trajectory detection and integration with DGIS/ATC/migration/economic policy.

Task Objective:
Implement unified evolution-risk scorer with explainability contract and confidence decomposition.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scorer combines drift, regime, hazard, and provenance features under a documented weighting policy; output includes stable explanation vectors and confidence intervals.

Expected Artifacts:
- `src/security/bpet/evolution_risk_scorer.rs`, `tests/conformance/bpet_risk_score_explainability.rs`, `artifacts/10.21/bpet_risk_score_catalog.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-1jpc/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_21/bd-1jpc/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.21] Implement unified evolution-risk scorer with explainability contract and confidence decomposition.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.21] Implement unified evolution-risk scorer with explainability contract and confidence decomposition." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.21] Implement unified evolution-risk scorer with explainability contract and confidence decomposition." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.21] Implement unified evolution-risk scorer with explainability contract and confidence decomposition." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.21] Implement unified evolution-risk scorer with explainability contract and confidence decomposition." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Scorer combines drift, regime, hazard, and provenance features under a documented weighting policy; output includes stable explanation vectors and confidence intervals.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:08.291306712Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:09:04.523267882Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T07:09:04.523240712Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-21","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jpc","depends_on_id":"bd-1b9x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpc","depends_on_id":"bd-2ao3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jpo","title":"[10.11] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging","description":"## Why This Exists

This is the section-wide verification gate for Section 10.11 (FrankenSQLite-Inspired Runtime Systems Integration Track). Section 10.11 applies runtime systems patterns inspired by SQLite's architecture to the franken_node product: capability narrowing, ambient-authority auditing, supervision trees, cancel/drain protocols, BOCPD regime detection, and VOI-budgeted monitoring. This gate ensures all 14 runtime hardening beads are validated.

Section 10.11 is where the "impossible-by-default" security model becomes concrete in product runtime behavior. Each bead implements a specific runtime discipline (capability narrowing, cancel protocols, checkpoint placement, supervision) that collectively make the product resilient to partial failures, resource exhaustion, and security boundary violations.

## What This Must Do

1. Aggregate verification evidence from all 14 Section 10.11 beads:
   - bd-cvt: Define capability profiles for product subsystems and enforce narrowing
   - bd-3vm: Add ambient-authority audit gate for product security-critical modules
   - bd-93k: Add checkpoint-placement contract in all long orchestration loops
   - bd-7om: Adopt canonical cancel -> drain -> finalize protocol contracts
   - bd-24k: Implement bounded masking helper for tiny atomic product operations
   - bd-2ah: Adopt canonical obligation-tracked two-phase channel contracts
   - bd-3he: Implement supervision tree with restart budgets and escalation policies
   - bd-2ko: Adopt canonical deterministic lab runtime and protocol scenario suites
   - bd-3u4: Implement BOCPD regime detector for workload/incident stream shifts
   - bd-2nt: Implement VOI-budgeted monitor scheduling for expensive diagnostics
   - bd-2gr: Integrate canonical monotonic security epochs and transition barriers
   - bd-3hw: Integrate canonical remote idempotency + saga semantics
   - bd-lus: Integrate canonical scheduler lane and global bulkhead policies
   - bd-390: Implement anti-entropy reconciliation for distributed product trust state
2. Verify capability narrowing coverage: every product subsystem has a declared capability profile.
3. Verify cancel/drain/finalize protocol adoption across all long-running operations.
4. Verify supervision tree coverage: all critical services have restart budgets and escalation paths.
5. Produce deterministic gate verdict.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 14 section beads must have PASS verdicts.
- Capability profile audit confirms 100% subsystem coverage with no ambient-authority violations.
- Cancel/drain/finalize protocol coverage audit confirms all long-running operations are covered.
- Supervision tree audit confirms all critical services have defined restart budgets.
- Gate verdict is deterministic and machine-readable.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Gate self-test with mock evidence.
- Structured logs: GATE_10_11_EVALUATION_STARTED, GATE_10_11_BEAD_CHECKED, GATE_10_11_CAPABILITY_AUDIT, GATE_10_11_VERDICT_EMITTED.

## Expected Artifacts

- `scripts/check_section_10_11_gate.py` — gate script with `--json` and `self_test()`
- `tests/test_check_section_10_11_gate.py` — unit tests
- `artifacts/section_10_11/bd-1jpo/verification_evidence.json`
- `artifacts/section_10_11/bd-1jpo/verification_summary.md`

## Dependencies

- Blocked by: bd-cvt, bd-3vm, bd-93k, bd-7om, bd-24k, bd-2ah, bd-3he, bd-2ko, bd-3u4, bd-2nt, bd-2gr, bd-3hw, bd-lus, bd-390, bd-1dpd, bd-2twu
- Blocks: bd-2j9w (program-wide gate), bd-20z (plan tracker)","acceptance_criteria":"AC for bd-1jpo (Section 10.11 Verification Gate):
1. A section-level test matrix covers every 10.11 deliverable (bd-cvt through bd-390) with happy-path, edge-case, and adversarial/error-path scenarios; the matrix is encoded as a machine-readable JSON mapping bead_id -> [test_ids].
2. E2E scripts exercise representative cross-cutting workflows: (a) full lifecycle from capability profile definition through anti-entropy convergence, (b) cancellation cascade through supervision tree with epoch transition mid-drain, (c) saga compensation under regime-change budget boost.
3. The test suite runs under the DeterministicRuntime (bd-2ko) with fixed seeds; CI runs each scenario twice and diffs outputs to verify bit-for-bit reproducibility.
4. Structured log validation: a log-schema checker verifies that every stable event code defined across all 10.11 beads (CAPABILITY_*, AMBIENT_*, CHECKPOINT_*, CANCEL_*, MASK_*, OBLIGATION_*, CHILD_*, SCENARIO_*, BOCPD_*, VOI_*, EPOCH_*, IDEMPOTENCY_*, SAGA_*, LANE_*, BULKHEAD_*, RECONCILIATION_*) appears in at least one test trace and conforms to the declared schema.
5. Coverage report: all Rust modules under the 10.11 scope achieve >= 80% line coverage; any module below threshold blocks the gate with COVERAGE_BELOW_THRESHOLD.
6. Gate verdict artifact at artifacts/section_10_11/bd-1jpo/verification_evidence.json encodes: per-bead pass/fail, coverage percentages, reproducibility check result, and log-schema conformance result.
7. The gate returns exit code 0 only when all sub-checks pass; any failure returns exit code 1 with a structured summary of which checks failed and why.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:07.460302460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T07:24:44.673107435Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T07:24:44.673000747Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-11","test-obligations","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-18sp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-1dpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-23x2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-24k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-2ah","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-2gr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-2ko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-2nt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-2twu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-390","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-3he","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-3hw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-3u4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-3vm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-7om","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-93k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-cvt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","depends_on_id":"bd-lus","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":54,"issue_id":"bd-1jpo","author":"RoseMountain","text":"Reopened  {"id":"bd-1jvh","title":"Harden lockstep temp-file cleanup in runtime harness","description":"Fix TOCTOU cleanup pattern in runtime/lockstep_harness TempFileCleanup drop path by removing exists-check race and validating cleanup behavior with focused unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticValley","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:41:48.807501352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T22:52:45.331826718Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T22:52:45.331799978Z","close_reason":"Implemented lockstep cleanup hardening + race test; validated with rch offloaded cargo test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
